title: Michael S Barr: Brief remarks on stablecoins
summary: Fed Governor Michael Barr delivered remarks on stablecoin regulation at The Federalist Society, discussing the GENIUS Act passed in 2025, which establishes a regulatory framework for stablecoin issuers by limiting permissible reserve assets to high-quality, highly liquid assets. Barr highlighted concerns about stablecoin vulnerabilities during market stress, drawing historical parallels to the Free Banking Era and the Panic of 1907, and emphasized that success will depend on regulatory implementation details including reserve asset regulation, anti-money-laundering controls, and capital and liquidity requirements.
key_points: The GENIUS Act (Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for U.S. Stablecoins) was passed by Congress in 2025, providing regulatory clarity for stablecoin issuers.
Stablecoins are currently primarily used for crypto-trading and as a dollar-denominated store of value in some foreign jurisdictions.
Barr cited historical parallels (Free Banking Era, Panic of 1907) to warn that private money created without sufficient safeguards can lead to bank runs and financial panics.
A key vulnerability is that stablecoin issuers have an incentive to maximize returns on reserve assets, which can increase profits in good times but risk undermining confidence during market stress.
The GENIUS Act's primary tool to mitigate run risk is limiting permissible reserve assets to an itemized list of high-quality, highly liquid assets.
Success of the regulatory framework will depend on implementation details including regulation of reserve assets, capital and liquidity requirements, and AML controls.
why_it_matters: This speech provides insight into how the Federal Reserve views stablecoin risks and the importance of the GENIUS Act's implementation. The speech signals regulatory focus on stablecoin reserve quality and liquidity as critical to financial stability, which could affect how stablecoin issuers structure their operations and reserves going forward. Market participants should monitor regulatory implementation for potential impacts on stablecoin market dynamics.
macro_context: The speech addresses financial stability risks associated with stablecoins, drawing connections to historical episodes of private money runs (Free Banking Era, Panic of 1907, money market fund pressures during GFC and COVID). Barr emphasizes that stablecoins will only be stable if they can be reliably redeemed at par during market stress, including episodes that pressure government debt and individual issuers.

issues_found: The GENIUS Act is referred to as being passed 'in 2025' in the summary and key points. The source states 'Last year, Congress passed the GENIUS Act' with the speech dated March 31, 2026, making this consistent but the year '2025' should be verified if the exact passage date is important for archival purposes.

notes: Speech delivered March 31, 2026, at The Federalist Society. Speaker is Michael S Barr, Member of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System. Note that while published by BIS, the views are those of the speaker and do not necessarily reflect BIS views. The speech does not signal directional monetary policy (hawkish/dovish) but focuses on regulatory policy for stablecoins.
